rationale: Title X is stronger overall because it states the comparison, setting, sample size, time horizon, and key findings: similar 12-month arrhythmia freedom with shorter procedures. It is long, but its specificity gives it higher editorial value for JICE.
loser_weakness: Title Y fits JICE well, but it is more generic. Phrases like "Feasibility, Safety, and 12-Month Efficacy" and "Multicenter Experience" do not communicate the main comparative result or the procedural advantage as directly.
